name: ivyhouse-terminal-fallback
description: On-demand legacy fallback adapter for Codex/Copilot terminal send, capture, verify, and bridge compatibility.

commands.title: IvyHouse Fallback: Start Codex + Copilot Terminals
IvyHouse Fallback: Send Text to Codex Terminal
IvyHouse Fallback: Send Text to Copilot Terminal

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.codexCommand.description: Command sent via sendText to start Codex CLI when fallback mode is active.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.copilotCommand.description: Command sent via sendText to start Copilot CLI when fallback mode is active.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.codexTerminalNa.description: Visible terminal name for the fallback Codex terminal.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.copilotTerminal.description: Visible terminal name for the fallback Copilot terminal.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.submitDelayMs.description: Delay in milliseconds between sendText and Enter submission in fallback mode.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.captureDir.description: Workspace-relative directory used for fallback capture artifacts.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.alwaysCaptureCo.description: Always write a rolling fallback capture of the Codex terminal to codex_live.txt.
Always write a rolling fallback capture of the Copilot terminal to copilot_live.txt.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.captureMaxSecon.description: Max seconds to capture terminal output after a fallback command.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.captureSilenceM.description: Stop fallback capture when no new output arrives for this long.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.captureMaxBytes.description: Max bytes written into a single fallback capture file.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.bridgePort.description: Localhost port used by the fallback bridge.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.bridgeMaxPayloa.description: Max payload size in bytes accepted by the fallback bridge /send endpoint.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.bridgeMaxReques.description: Max request body size in bytes accepted by the fallback bridge.

ivyhouseTerminalFallback.bridgeRateLimit.description: Fallback bridge token-bucket refill rate per second.
